humanities_role_explanation: Evaluates computational systems on retrieving and identifying linguistic wordplay and puns (content), with the goal of aiding humor scholars, translators, and language learners (domain).
benchmark_modalities_explanation: A text-only information retrieval setup comprising search queries and document collections in both English and European Portuguese.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Evaluating whether systems can retrieve text documents that satisfy dual constraints: topical relevance to a semantic query, and the presence of rhetorical/linguistic wordplay (puns).

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Specifically designed for humor-aware information retrieval and linguistic wordplay analysis.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluated systems include the Qwen LLM family (Qwen-14B, Qwen-32B, Qwen-4B, Qwen-8B). Additionally, the dataset incorporates synthetic texts generated by Claude, ChatGPT, Bard, Llama-2, and Phi-3 Mini.

llm_is_primary_system.note: LLMs are the primary systems evaluated in the top-performing pipelines for retrieval, filtering, and humor classification.

quantitative_metrics.note: MAP, GMAP, P@R, MRR, P@5, P@10, P@100, P@1000, NDCG@5, and bpref

english_language.note: The paper and a primary portion of the evaluated corpus are in English.

summary: The paper presents Task 1 of the CLEF 2025 JOKER track, which focuses on humor-aware information retrieval. It introduces multilingual test collections in English and European Portuguese, comprised of mixed human-written and machine-generated texts, to evaluate retrieval systems on finding topically relevant puns and wordplay.
key_findings: The best results were achieved by team pjmathematician using a two-stage Qwen LLM pipeline (MAP of 0.3501 on English) and team rasion using dense retrieval with a RoBERTa-based classifier (MAP of 0.4051 on Portuguese), vastly outperforming traditional BM25/RM3 baselines and cross-encoders.
